register_definition.name: clinical_dissociative
register_definition.description: Controlled, precise, slightly detached first-person present-tense narration. Interiority expressed through observation and physical response, never direct psychological labelling.
markers: Observation-first interiority (physical sensation over emotion naming)
Controlled sentence structure that fractures under pressure
Clinical detachment in describing emotionally charged events
Sensory dissociation (time distortion, auditory lag, visual delay)

deflection.description: Avoidant defense mechanisms redirecting attention away from traumatic triggers or psychological breakdown

examples.label: Forced brightness pivot
examples.context: Chapter 4 — Lily confronts Ashley after boutique outburst
examples.text: Lily studies me. "Are you okay?" "Yeah. I'll be even better once I get a scoop or two." She hesitates, her hand on the handle, her voice gentle but intruding. "You wanna talk about what just happened in there?" I pretend ignorance. "What's that?" "Are you serious?" She lets out a breath. "You went off on that woman. I've never seen you that angry." ... "She was a nosy old lady, Lily. Anyone would've snapped." ... "Drop it, goddammit!"

examples.label: Rationalization of dissociative behavior
examples.context: Detective Reeves notices table set for two
examples.text: "But what caught Reeves's attention was the dining table. It was set for two. Two place settings, two coffee cups, two napkins folded precisely... 'You entertaining?' Reeves asked casually... 'No. I... like the symmetry,' Ashley said. Her voice was tight."

examples.label: Dismissal of auditory/verbal dissociation
examples.context: Lily catches Ashley talking to Elle
examples.text: "Lily's gaze drifts from me to the empty side of the bed. 'Sounded like someone.' 'Just… thinking out loud.' She hesitates... Lily's eyes dart to the empty space. 'Ash… who are you looking at?' 'No one,' I whisper."

deception.description: Active maintenance of a false narrative to preserve the wall between the constructed self and the truth of the past

examples.label: Polished false identity
examples.context: Lunch with Arden's family — practiced lie about parents
examples.text: "I force a smile. 'They'll... love that.' But my pulse is thundering, and the chandelier above me flickers... A lie, polished smooth with practice. It's strange how easily it comes now, how 'they're gone' breathes for me. A small lie that fences my worlds apart: the bright one I've built with Arden, and the dark one I barely crawled out of. Because the truth would drag Clara into this room, and Clara… Clara would set everything on fire."

examples.label: Systematic parental deception
examples.context: Clara Whitmore describes drugging her child
examples.text: "I never saw her take medication," I say. "She hated pills." Mom looks down at her cup. "She didn't know. I put it in her food," she continues. "The doctor said deception was better than confrontation. She wasn't old enough to understand a trigger, but it kept the episodes quiet. She was stable."

examples.label: Performance of friendly closure
examples.context: Public interview post-divorce — sensory dissociation while lying
examples.text: "I'm in a tailored ivory jumpsuit... on the monitor across the studio, my reflection is a half-second off, smiling when I haven't yet. ... 'Arden's doing just fine,' I say, smoothing my palms against my thighs. ... Tessa beams. 'So no hard feelings.' 'None,' I say. 'Just perspective, and good timing.' ... The audience claps, but I don't hear it the right way. It comes in waves, too slow, like a heartbeat not my own."

collapse.description: Total failure of dissociative defenses where compartmentalization breaks down and suppressed trauma floods consciousness

examples.label: Full dissociative collapse
examples.context: Chapter 43 — mother reveals truth of identity
examples.text: Something inside gives way. My shoulders slump. Energy leaks out at once. I'm too empty to rage, too heavy to lift my head. Then it hits all at once. Everything I've held rigid for seventeen years shatters. The sob starts small, grows raw, animalistic. My throat burns. ... Crying from somewhere under the ribs, where grief has been hiding too long. ... I don't fight anymore. I let it wreck me. The guilt. The loss. The impossible relief of finally remembering.

examples.label: Immediate internal mental collapse
examples.context: Direct aftermath of traumatic event — agency shutdown
examples.text: "The gun lies where it fell, metal winking in the weak light. A dark pool spreads from the body beside it, finding the seams in the floorboards. My breath hitches. Pulse slams in my ears. I can't move. I can't think. Only feel the world collapsing inward. Can I ever make this right?"

examples.label: Clinical observation of dissociative routine
examples.context: Detective Reeves observes table set for absent guest
examples.text: "But what caught Reeves's attention was the dining table. It was set for two. Two place settings, two coffee cups, two napkins folded precisely. A plate of fruit sat in the center, apples, grapes, tangerines. None of it looked recently eaten... Reeves made a mental note: Possible dissociative behavior. Setting table for an absent guest. Or a ghost."

examples.label: Formal identification of identity collapse
examples.context: Interrogation — self fragments into distinct personalities
examples.text: "I don't remember," she said. "But I think I did. Or... she did." "Who's 'she'?" Ashley didn't answer. She was staring at something behind Reeves, at a point in empty space where nothing existed... Reeves made a note: Possible DID (Dissociative Identity Disorder). Requires psychiatric evaluation.

pressure_states.description: Voice register must be demonstrated across all pressure states to pass the gate

states.label: Baseline / Safe
states.description: Character at rest — controlled, observational, precise

states.label: Social Performance
states.description: Character maintaining facade in public — heightened control, micro-fractures visible

states.label: Under Confrontation
states.description: Character challenged directly — deflection patterns activate, sentence structure shifts

states.label: Internal Crisis
states.description: Dissociative defense straining — sensory distortion, temporal displacement

states.label: Full Collapse
states.description: Defense failure — raw, uncontrolled, animalistic register

arc_points.description: Protagonist arc coherence check — wound must be rendered, tested under pressure, and transformed

questions.question: Is the character's core wound clearly rendered through behavior and physical response (not stated)?
Does the wound visibly distort the character's behavior under pressure (deflection, deception, collapse)?
Is there evidence of transformation potential — the wound's grip loosening or tightening across corpus samples?
